title: blumea crispata
intro: Blumea crispata, also known as wild chamomile or chamomile daisy, is a perennial herb native to Africa and Asia, belonging to the daisy family Asteraceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Blumea crispata include reducing anxiety and stress, promoting relaxation, and soothing digestive issues, as well as its potential to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi. Herbal preparations of Blumea crispata typically involve infusing the leaves in hot water to make a tea, or using the dried flowers in a calming tea blend.
